TWO SPOTS ON MT HIGHWAY-13N AT MILE MARKERS 11.5 AND 12.5 HAD WATER GOING OVER THE ROAD, IN AREAS WITH DEEP COULEES. A RANCHER IN THE AREA STATED HE HAD NEVER SEEN WATER OVER THE ROAD IN THESE LOCATIONS IN HIS 85 YEARS.
SPOTTER REPORT 1.12 INCHES OF RAINFALL, NO HAIL. WIND DAMAGE OBSERVED TO A FEW SHINGLES AND THE SOFFIT BROKE LOOSE. NO MAJOR TREE OR CROP DAMAGE OBSERVED SO FAR. TIME ESTIMATED.

STRAIGHT LINE WINDS HIT IN THE TOWN OF FAIRVIEW. PHOTOS INCLUDE LARGE SPRUCE AND COTTONWOOD TREES UPROOTED, A METAL BUILDING CAVED IN AND LARGE TREE BRANCHES DOWN. RICHLAND COUNTY DES SURVEYED THE DAMAGE THE NEXT MORNING AND INDICATED ALL THE DEBRIS WAS LAYING IN THE SAME DIRECTION. WIND SPEED ESTIMATES ARE 75 TO 85 MPH.

SEVERAL POWER POLES (BOTH LOCAL ELECTRIC COOPERATIVE AND DOE ONES) WERE SNAPPED AT THE BASES IN THIS AREA. A MEASURED GUST IN THE AREA WAS 79 MPH ON A HOME WEATHER STATION. THESE ARE NEWER POLES THAT HAVE BEEN REPLACED IN RECENT YEARS. WIND SPEEDS ARE ESTIMATED AT 100 TO 105 MPH.
THIS GUST IS LIKELY ENHANCED BY THE COLD FRONT COMING THROUGH AS WELL, THE STORM IS MOSTLY SOUTH OF THIS AREA.
THUNDERSTORM WINDS BROKE WINDOWS, DAMAGED CROPS AND GARDEN, AND TOPPLED PINE TREES. FROM SOCIAL MEDIA.

NUMEROUS POWER POLES FROM COUNTY ROAD 201 SOUTHWARD ON COUNTY ROAD 328 WERE SNAPPED AT THEIR BASES FOR UP TO 6 MILES ALONG THE ROAD. THERE WERE A FEW SNAPPED ABOUT 1/3 OF THE WAY UP, BUT 98% WERE AT THE BASES. WIND SPEED ESTIMATES ARE 95 TO 100 MPH.

SEVERAL BUILDINGS IN THE COMMUNITY OF LAMBERT, MT SUSTAINED DAMAGE DUE TO SEVERE THUNDERSTORM WINDS AND VERY LARGE HAIL. MOST HOMES WERE NEWER AND WELL BUILT. SOME SIDING WAS STRIPPED FROM THE SOUTHWEST CORNERS OF BUILDINGS. VINYL SIDING TOOK A HIT FROM STRONG WINDS (MEASURED NEARBY AT 71 MPH) AND HAIL THAT WAS AT LEAST ONE INCH IN DIAMETER AND MAYBE GREATER.
VINYL SIDING ON SEVERAL HOMES TOOK A HIT FROM STRONG WINDS (MEASURED NEARBY AT 71 MPH) AND HAIL THAT WAS AT LEAST ONE INCH IN DIAMETER AND MAYBE GREATER.

NEARLY A MILE OF POWER LINES WERE SNAPPED FROM 1/3 TO 1/2 WAY UP OFF THE GROUND FROM COUNTY ROAD 130 TO COUNTY ROAD 129. A NEWER METAL BUILDING IN THE AREA HAD THE SW PORTION OF THE ROOF FAIL AND COLLAPSE/GET BLOWN TO THE NORTHEAST. WIND ESTIMATES IN THIS LOCATION RANGE FROM 110 TO 115 MPH. A MESONET STATION WITH 3 MILES OF THIS LOCATION MEASURED 90 MPH.
